title: MeetSpace
text:
Adam Hardaker
    
  
  
    Home
    Research
    Side Quests
    CV
    Email
  





MeetSpace is a lightweight campus events board built with React, Vite, Tailwind, and Supabase. It supports student and organization posts, filtering, bookmarks, reporting, admin review, and profile tools.

The visual style is deliberately more playful than this site: grid paper, sticker shadows, tape labels, and hand-drawn card treatment.
Visit MeetSpace | Intro video | GitHub

title: Adam Hardaker
text:
Selected Work


Climate behavior interventions and publication bias
Reanalysis of 144 effect estimates from 91 studies using Robust Bayesian Meta-Analysis. The published evidence strongly favors a zero average effect after accounting for publication bias and model uncertainty.
PNAS Nexus | Code and materials


Academic mobility and research careers
Doctoral work at INCHER linking dissertations, publications, and institutional records to study movement through higher education systems.


MeetSpace
A React, Vite, Tailwind, and Supabase campus events app with playful bulletin-board styling, filtering, reporting, profiles, and organization workflows.
meet-space.com | GitHub

title: Adam Hardaker
text:
A Bit More Human
Before academia, I spent eight years as a U.S. Army aviation officer and Blackhawk pilot, followed by a transition into business development. I’ve found that the leadership and operational focus from the military, combined with the strategic mindset of business development, translates surprisingly well to managing complex data projects and research pipelines. These days I split time between research, programming, Rugby Cassel admin, and the occasional side project that starts as “this should exist” and becomes a repo.

title: About Me
text:
Adam Hardaker
    
  
  
    Home
    Research
    Side Quests
    CV
    Email
  





I am a doctoral researcher at the University of Kassel’s International Center for Higher Education Research (INCHER), where I study the economics of academic mobility and the systems that shape research careers. My work combines data engineering, econometrics, and machine learning, building large-scale pipelines that link dissertations, publication databases, and institutional records across Europe.
Before academia, I spent eight years as a U.S. Army aviation officer and Blackhawk pilot. Since then, I have worked at the intersection of research and technology, from building decentralized startup programs at the IOTA Foundation to developing entity-resolution models for million-record datasets.
Outside of research, I can be found administrating Rugby Cassel as club president, tinkering with programming projects, or trying to make practical tools that reduce coordination friction for real people.

Adam Hardaker


m.a.hardaker@proton.me Kassel, Germany



RESEARCH FOCUS
Causal Machine Learning (Double/Debiased Machine Learning), Bibliometrics, Economics of Innovation and Higher Education, Academic Mobility


PUBLICATIONS
No evidence that non-incentivized behavioral interventions effectively mitigate climate change after adjusting for publication bias
Hardaker, A., Asanov, I., Bartoš, F., & Bruns, S. B. PNAS Nexus, 2026; https://doi.org/10.1093/pnasnexus/pgag150


PRESENTATIONS

06/2025 2025 Reading Group in Innovation Studies (REGIS), Bordeaux
09/2025 10th Summer School on Data and Algorithms for ST&I Studies, KU Leuven
11/2025 Causal Data Science Meeting, Copenhagen Business School
06/2026 DRUID26 Conference, Copenhagen Business School



EMPLOYMENT
University of Kassel
Doctoral Researcher | May 2025 - Present | Kassel, Germany

Conduct research on academic labor markets, international mobility, and higher education systems using large-scale linked datasets.
Built an end‑to‑end, modular R pipeline to harmonize and link 1996–2025 academic rosters with multi‑country thesis datasets, delivering a single longitudinal entity store.
Designed a scalable AI/ML entity‑resolution pipeline to create longitudinal academic record data from complex 1M+ observation data sources.
Implemented university name resolution with per‑country rules plus ROR references; produced unmatched reports and achieved high institution coverage across 1M+ EDT records in 8+ countries.
Delivered QA/ops tooling: scraping + ingestion jobs, 5 Shiny applications for hand-labelling, linkage-review, visualisation, data coverage, and model evaluation.

IOTA Foundation
Research Analyst & Business Developer | Feb 2022 - Jul 2023 | Berlin, Germany

Spearheaded ecosystem growth initiatives, onboarding 30+ projects to a distributed ledger platform.
Created and led “Touchpoint”, a global program supporting startups in decentralized technology.
Managed 70+ strategic relationships across exchanges, investors, and technology partners, driving 40% ecosystem growth.
Conducted market research and operational analysis to support growth and product strategy.
Presented on distributed systems and emerging financial technologies at international conferences.

U.S. Army
Aviation Maintenance Officer & UH-60A/L Blackhawk Pilot | Dec 2010 - Jun 2018 | South Korea, Europe, USA

Led operations, logistics, and training functions across aviation and maintenance organizations.
Managed multinational military units of up to 200 personnel in high-responsibility operational environments.
Directed aviation maintenance and readiness workflows supporting fleets of up to 100 aircraft.
Planned and executed training programs, operational reporting, and mission coordination under time-sensitive conditions.
Maintained a spotless safety record while executing 30+ high-priority missions.



EDUCATION
University of Kassel
Master of Science; Economic Behavior and Governance | Mar 2025 | Kassel, Germany

GPA: 4.0 (1.2 German scale); Thesis: Leveraging Double/Debiased Machine Learning to Improve Meta-Analyses

Bard College Berlin
Bachelor of Arts; Economics, Politics, and Social Thought | May 2022 | Berlin, Germany

Awards: DAAD Prize, Best Thesis Prize; GPA: 4.0 (1.0 German scale)

University of Florida
Bachelor of Science; Agriculture Operations Management | Dec 2010 | Gainesville, Florida, USA


OTHER

Technical Skills: R, Python, SQL, FastAPI, Git, Stata, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Causal Inference, Machine Learning, Statistical Modelling
Languages: English (Native), German (B2)
Leadership: President of Rugby Cassel (operations, recruitment, web development)
Independent Project: Founder & developer of MeetSpace, an event aggregation platform for scalable local discovery. Developed backend with Python, FastAPI & SQLite.

title: Research
text:
Adam Hardaker
    
  
  
    Home
    Research
    Side Quests
    CV
    Email
I work on higher education, academic labor markets, causal machine learning, and meta-research. I am especially interested in what we can learn when messy administrative, bibliometric, and institutional data are linked carefully.

title: Research
text:
Publications

Hardaker, A., Asanov, I., Bartos, F., & Bruns, S. B. (2026). No evidence that non-incentivized behavioral interventions effectively mitigate climate change after adjusting for publication bias. PNAS Nexus, pgag150. https://doi.org/10.1093/pnasnexus/pgag150 | Code and materials
Earlier version: I4R Discussion Paper Series No. 263.

title: Research
text:
Working Areas


Academic Careers
Dissertations, publications, mobility, and institutional trajectories in European higher education systems.


Methods
Causal machine learning, entity resolution, econometrics, and reproducible data pipelines.


Meta-Research
Publication bias, robustness, and how evidence changes when selective reporting is modeled explicitly.

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Professional Summary
I am a doctoral researcher at INCHER, University of Kassel, studying academic mobility, research careers, and the systems that shape higher education. My background combines eight years of leadership as a U.S. Army aviation officer and Blackhawk pilot, experience in business development, and deep technical expertise in data engineering, causal machine learning, and econometrics.
